title: viola pseudograeca
intro: Viola pseudograeca, also known as the "Lesser Dog-violet", is a perennial plant species belonging to the violet family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Viola pseudegraeca include treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as providing relief from sore throats and mouth ulcers. Viola pseudograeca can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
